text: [Music]
Nintendo's latest weird game fitness
thing ring fit adventure has been a lot
more difficult than I expected
not because it's attempted integrating
Fitness into an RPG is too complicated
or obtuse but because it's
honest-to-goodness workout is kicking my
butt for the past two weeks I've spent a
daily session not much longer than my
usual regular gym time with it and it
almost always leaves me sweating but
it's a challenge I'm excited to face
every day even when I'm not feeling it's
adventure elements as much as that burn
[Music]
the ring fit is basically a Pilates ring
and you can use it as a more traditional
fitness device with custom workout
routines and QuickPlay challenges built
into ring fit adventure but it's the
surprisingly lengthy adventure mode
campaign that serves as the main
attraction it actually does a decent job
of adapting Fitness ideas into an RPG
adventure played about at 30 minutes to
an hour at a time that said this is a
clear attempt to be an RPG that can be
played by even those who have never
heard of Final Fantasy so don't expect a
lot of depth to its mechanics the story
starts off with a page straight out of
RPG cliches 101 your hero is tricked
into unleashing a buff bodybuilding
dragon named Drago who plans to unleash
his evil upon the land he was imprisoned
by his friend ring who now is your
fitness companion slash training partner
on a journey to thwart Drago's plan
which we can only assume was to make
everybody unfit or maybe buff it's not
entirely clear seeing the end of the
story seems like it take almost another
month of workouts but that's not what's
driving me to continue ring fit tosses
out that kind of RPG ism like they're
about to be banned and that's doubly
disappointing because it's on the cusp
of being a truly interesting spin on the
genre instead of a weak parody of it
it's pun filled world has a land called
sport ax and characters like AB Donais
which I admit are pretty good and there
are some clever character designs based
on anthropomorphize to exercise
equipment but it's dialogue is
repetitive boring or even pretty awkward
which steps on just about every amusing
moment trying to squeeze me really hard
ring is the only voice character and he
does a great job of being a fitness
enthusiast pushing you along to do one
more squat or ring press but he lacks a
fun foil to bounce off the world itself
is colorful but similarly empty and
lifeless even though the upbeat
soundtrack does its best
[Music]
of course what we're all here for is the
exercise based combat and ring fit
adventure largely delivers on that idea
the fitness role-playing game or F RPG
as I insist on calling it is a sweaty
challenge and the level of customization
that quickly opens up after the first
world or to let me tune it to my fitness
level surprisingly well
ring fits battle system is its most
inventive twist and after a slow start
it sticks the landing at first the
campaign gives you far too few moves to
work with and so I ended up doing a lot
more squats in my first couple sessions
than I would have chosen to it was a
huge relief to my thighs when new ones
began to be routinely introduced though
allowing you to customize your move set
before going into each level outside a
variety having a good number of moves
available allows for more targeted
exercises so I could enter a handful of
levels and specifically choose moves to
focus on my core or arms now that my
legs were jelly all of these exercises
are performed using the ring fits
titular ring and the leg band which must
be pretty sad that it didn't get top
billing
by measuring feedback from each joint
con ring fit pulls off a surprising
level of finesse gauging when I haven't
quite stressed my yoga pose far enough
or squatted low enough you can totally
trick it but if you behave yourself it
does an impressive job of rewarding you
accurately
[Music]
while I've mostly gotten by sticking to
the most powerful handful of attacks
some of ring fits design ideas like
color matching attack types to the
various Fitness themed enemies can make
all the difference and the gradual
addition of a skill tree new outfits to
upgrade your attack and defense that's
and more
I haven't found myself bored by combat
or limited in my exercise opportunities
on the other hand I was caught off guard
by how much of an emphasis rincon
adventure places on it stand in for
potions which are smoothies and soups I
have had to use smoothies regularly
throughout almost every world to survive
some of the toughest challenges and if
anything ring fit pulls off the
impressive challenge of making another
RPG cliche level grinding feel vital and
worthwhile because running through
levels and fighting enemies is a
physically involving and rewarding
experience
[Music]
ringg fin adventure is a marathon not a
sprint
just like adapting a healthy lifestyle
should be the last couple of weeks
haven't altered my life or made me look
like the rock all of a sudden but as a
way of getting into or back into in my
case a pattern of healthy living ring
fit has been a solid catalyst and after
about 18 hours and more than half the
adventure done I fully expect Heat
playing as the week's go on that's not
to see where ring undergoes week story
goes but rather to keep engaging with
the fun and challenging blend of
exercise and RPG mechanics for more on
everything Nintendo stay tuned to our
weekly show Nintendo voice chat for all
the latest greatest news previews and
reviews and for everything else Nintendo
you're already in the right place idea
[Music]
